design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate downhill slalom courses by dodging obstacles and passing through flags within time limits, earning points through successful tricks on moguls to unlock solo runs with extended timers.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Speed versus precision: maintaining momentum to beat the clock while accurately threading through gates and avoiding obstacles without tumbling.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience the thrill of downhill skiing with risk-reward trick mechanics, progressing from beginner to expert slopes.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Slalom commits to physics-based skiing mechanics where gate placement and obstacle avoidance directly impact speed (snowplowing penalty for wrong-side flag passes). The game refuses to abstract these costs—players must internalize the spatial and momentum implications of their line choices. Trick system emphasizes optional expression over mandatory optimization, rewarding precision without punishing conservative play.

difficulty.continues_system: Three mountain difficulty tiers selectable at start: Snowy Hill (beginner), Steep Peak (intermediate), Mount Nasty (expert). 24 total runs distributed across three mountains.
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-run time limits; qualifying runs grant solo runs with timer extensions based on points earned. High scores saved to memory between sessions.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players select their mountain tier upfront, determining overall difficulty trajectory. Performance on qualifying runs directly affects solo run difficulty (timer length scales with earned points). Optional trick system allows risk-taking for bonus points or conservative play for consistent time management.

progression.upgrade_system: Performance-based: Points earned in qualifying runs convert to additional seconds on solo run timers, creating a soft unlock mechanism where player skill directly enables access to longer solo challenges.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Solo runs unlock based on earning any points in qualifying runs. Difficulty tier (mountain) selection gates overall progression difficulty from the start.
replayability_hooks: High score tracking persisted to memory encourages replay for score improvement
Three selectable difficulty mountains provide distinct progression paths
Trick system enables skill expression with variable point rewards
Solo run timer extensions reward performance, creating emergent difficulty scaling

world_building_techniques: Mountain naming scheme (Snowy Hill, Steep Peak, Mount Nasty) conveys difficulty progression through environmental context
Obstacle variety (trees, flags, snowmen, sledders, other skiers) creates sense of populated ski resort environment

environmental_storytelling: Mountain progression (beginner to expert slopes) narrativizes player skill growth
Obstacle placement and density increase with difficulty tier, making world feel progressively more hazardous
